items.title: Random forest training and aggregation
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  data["training data D"] --> boot1["bootstrap D1"]
  data --> boot2["bootstrap D2"]
  data --> bootB["bootstrap DB"]
  boot1 --> tree1["tree 1 with random feature splits"]
  boot2 --> tree2["tree 2 with random feature splits"]
  bootB --> treeB["tree B with random feature splits"]
  tree1 --> agg["aggregate: mean or majority vote"]
  tree2 --> agg
  treeB --> agg
  agg --> pred["final prediction"]
items.caption: Each tree sees a bootstrap sample and random feature subsets at splits; predictions are aggregated.

items.title: Bagging vs random feature subsampling
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  bag["bagging: different row samples"] --> decorr["trees still correlated if same top feature wins every split"]
  feat["random max_features per split"] --> decorr2["trees explore different paths"]
  decorr2 --> avg["averaging lowers variance"]
items.caption: Bagging diversifies rows; feature subsampling diversifies splits—both needed for effective decorrelation.
